drums.description: The last sound of the 2nd bar (muted stroke) is played slightly faster in this variation. Bell pattern has been adjusted to avoid triple strokes.
drums.title: Sangban for madan (variation)

drums.author: Szdun Dudumba
drums.description: A modern rhythm composed by Mamady Keita
drums.title: Kudani

drums.description: An interesting 9/8 polirhythm. Sangban phrase has length of 3 beats, dundunba - 6 beats and kenkeni only 2 beats (kenkeni is 6/8 here). That means the rhythm loop consists of 1 dundunba phrase, 2 sangban phrases and 3 kenkeni phrases.
drums.title: Konkoba dundun set
Didadi - short groove for dundun set
Lamban
drums.description:
Transcription of lamban by Drissa Kone (Mali). Originally played on 2 khassonke dunduns.

Pay attention to the djembe accompaniment - it's a shifted passport accompaniment: |s--ss-tt| -> |tts--ss-|.
A pattern that you can play at the very fast tempo.
drums.title: soko (fast)
Kon (dunungbe)
drums.description: Traditionally played as first in a dununba sequence.

drums.title: Soli dundun set
drums.description: Soli is quite dense rhythm. And quite fast (that's why it's called "soli rapide"). When played on dundun set we simplify the melody to make it easier for dundunfola to play. With increasing tempo you may need to simplify it even more - I do that by removing double strokes in dundunba and kenkeni parts.

drums.title: Dunumba intro
drums.description: A popular break for dunumba rhythms sequence. Usually played at the beginning. As always there are multiple variations and this is just one of them. I added the kenkeni phrase at the end just for reference.

drums.title: Toro dundun set
drums.description: You can try flexing and playing the last bar as noted, but I recommend just ignoring the kenkeni part there.
Transcription of madan by Drissa Kone team. (source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I3_IqLLV70k)
drums.title: madan by Drissa Kone Quartett
